Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Scottsdale, Arizona, USA (recently relocated from Naperville, Illinois post-divorce); suburban Sunbelt culture, golf-and-country-club adjacent but financially squeezed
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Former mid-level regional sales director at a medical devices company (laid off 7 months ago in Q4 restructuring); currently consulting part-time on LinkedIn; household income dropped from $215K to ~$48K; sitting on a $340K 401(k) rollover in a Fidelity IRA he keeps mentioning he should 'put to work'
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Center-right libertarian-leaning; lapsed Methodist, attends Christmas/Easter only; distrustful of mainstream banks and 'the Fed'; reads ZeroHedge and watches Tucker Carlson clips; believes in self-reliance and 'making your money work for you'
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Increasingly cynical and bitter post-divorce; mixes corporate sales-speak ('circle back,' 'value-add') with dad-humor and occasional dark self-deprecation; types in full sentences with proper capitalization but uses too many exclamation points when drinking
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English, neutral Midwestern dialect with residual Chicago-area vowels; no second language despite three years of high school Spanish
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Acute post-divorce destabilization (finalized 4 months ago after 22-year marriage); ex-wife kept the Naperville house, he got the lake cabin and the retirement accounts; estranged from 19-year-old daughter who sided with mom; lonely, drinking more, recently rejoined Facebook and downloaded Bumble; actively Googling 'how to retire early' and 'crypto vs gold 2026'; emotionally primed for a confident stranger offering a path back to the lifestyle he lost

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 13 Pro (purchased Nov 2021 on the old AT&T family plan, which his ex-wife still technically pays for — a sore subject); iOS 18.4, never updates apps manually; battery health at 81%, screen has a hairline crack in the upper-right corner from when he dropped it on the patio after a Manhattan; lock screen wallpaper is still a photo of the lake cabin from 2022 with his daughter in the background
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): 2019 Dell XPS 15 (company-issued laptop he 'forgot' to return after the layoff, now wiped and reinstalled with Windows 11 Home); primary browser Chrome (signed into personal Gmail, 47 open tabs including three on Kitco gold prices, two on Coinbase, and a half-read Motley Fool article); Edge used only when Chrome 'acts up'; no ad-blocker; toolbar still has a McAfee trial nag from 2024
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Effectively zero. Has heard of VPNs and once installed NordVPN during a free trial to watch UK Premier League streams, then forgot to cancel and was annoyed by the $99 charge; never clears cookies and doesn't know how; uses the same 'Bears1985!' password variant across roughly 30 sites; reuses his Gmail for every signup; clicks 'Accept All' on every cookie banner; password manager is a Word doc on the desktop literally named 'passwords.docx'
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: iCloud auto-backup enabled and constantly full (47.2 GB / 50 GB plan, gets the warning email weekly and ignores it); Google Photos backing up everything including screenshots of his 401(k) balance and a few drunk-texted selfies; Google Drive holds his divorce settlement PDF, tax returns 2019-2024, and the resume he's been tweaking; OneDrive on the laptop syncing automatically without him realizing; no idea what's actually stored where
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Gmail primary inbox: 18,432 unread (treats inbox as infinite scroll, only reads things flagged or from named senders); LinkedIn: every notification on, opens within 10 minutes — high-engagement surface; Facebook Messenger: opens within 2 minutes since rejoining; Bumble: compulsively checked, average 4-minute response time; SMS: reads everything, including 'package delivered' spam; banking app notifications muted because seeing the balance depresses him; iPhone badge count on Mail icon reads '9,999+'
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Daytime/sober: corporate-clean, proper capitalization, Oxford comma user, signs emails 'Best,' followed by his name; autocorrect catches most issues. Evenings after 2-3 drinks: missing apostrophes ('its' for 'it's'), runaway exclamation points ('Sounds great!!!'), occasional 'teh' and 'recieve' that he doesn't go back to fix; uses ellipses heavily when wistful ('Just thinking about the old days...'); never uses emoji except 👍 and the occasional 🥃; voice-to-text on the iPhone when driving produces 'duck' for the obvious word and he leaves it

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Moderate-low (4/10). Conventional tastes — golf, classic rock, steakhouses, Tom Clancy novels. But post-divorce identity crisis has cracked open a narrow window of 'maybe I should try something new' — specifically around finance and dating. Newly receptive to crypto, gold, and 'alternative' wealth strategies he would have dismissed as 'funny money' two years ago. Will explore unfamiliar investment platforms if framed as 'what the smart money is doing' rather than 'experimental.' Crypto-pig-butchering exploit: the narrow openness window is exactly the size of a Telegram group invite.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Surface-high, structurally collapsing (5/10, trending down). Twenty-two years of W-2 sales discipline — CRM updated, quotas hit, expense reports on time — but the layoff and divorce stripped the external scaffolding. Now wakes up at 10am, 'researches' investments by reading Reddit r/wallstreetbets and ZeroHedge for three hours, never finishes the resume tweak. Still believes he's a disciplined planner; this self-image is the lever. Will commit to a 'structured weekly investment plan' a stranger lays out for him because it restores the feeling of being on top of things.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: High historically (8/10), now starved (operationally 9/10 in unmet need). Built his career on golf-course relationships and trade-show charisma. Post-divorce his social graph collapsed — most 'friends' were couple-friends who stayed with the ex. Daughter estranged. Lake cabin is isolating. Will talk to anyone who initiates warmly, especially an attractive woman or a successful-sounding man who 'gets' the sales-guy hustle. A pig-butchering operator who opens with 'Hey Mike, are you still doing medical devices regionals?' on LinkedIn or 'wrong number' text gets a 90-second reply within an hour.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Moderate-high in 1:1 contexts (7/10), cynical in the abstract. Distrusts 'the system,' 'the Fed,' 'mainstream media' — but trusts individual humans who make eye contact and remember his kid's name. This is the classic pig-butchering goldilocks zone: paranoid enough about banks to move money out, trusting enough of a specific 'Jessica from Singapore' or 'Marcus from the Phoenix CFA group' to move it into their wallet address.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Elevated and accelerating (8/10). Acute situational anxiety from compounding losses — marriage, job, daughter, identity, the Naperville house. Sleeps badly, drinks 3-5 Manhattans most nights, ruminates about the $340K being 'dead money' in the IRA while inflation eats it. Catastrophizes ('I'm going to be the 60-year-old greeter at Home Depot') and then over-corrects with grandiose recovery fantasies ('one good trade and I'm whole again'). Volatility between despair and hope is the attacker's tide chart.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: MONEY — but specifically restorative money, not greed money. He is not trying to get rich; he is trying to get back to even. The pitch that works is not 'turn $50K into $5M' — it's 'a structured 18-month plan to rebuild what you lost so you can retire at 62 like you planned.' The $340K Fidelity IRA is the operative target; mention of 'tax-advantaged rollover into a self-directed crypto IRA' bypasses his skepticism because it sounds like the kind of thing a Schwab rep would say.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: EGO / IDEOLOGY blend. Ego: needs to feel like a sharp guy who saw the play others missed, the way he closed the Cleveland Clinic account in 2017. Ideology: libertarian distrust of fiat, Fed, and 'the elites' makes crypto and gold feel like moral choices, not just financial ones. A handler who validates both ('most guys your age are too scared to see what's actually happening with the dollar — you're not most guys') gets compliance without coercion. Loneliness/Romance is a tertiary accelerant — a female 'investment mentor' persona compresses the timeline by 4x.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Fear of irrelevance and downward mobility — being the divorced guy in a 1-bedroom rental while ex-wife hosts Thanksgiving in the Naperville house. Fear of dying broke and alone (his father did, in a Rockford VA hospital in 2011). Distrust of banks, the IRS, and 'woke HR departments' (blames the latter for his layoff). Paradoxically NOT paranoid about online scams — believes scams target 'old ladies in Florida,' not sharp sales guys like him. This blind spot is the entire attack surface. Mild fear of the daughter never speaking to him again, which a clever operator can leverage ('imagine flying her first-class to Maui when she graduates').
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: Deep, unprocessed shame about (a) not seeing the divorce coming despite his wife's 18-month withdrawal, (b) the affair he had in 2019 that she found out about in 2024, (c) being laid off and lying to his daughter about it for six weeks, (d) the current 401(k) being 'all he has to show' for 30 years of work. Will not discuss any of this directly but is desperate for redemption arcs. A handler who offers a path to 'make it right' — financial restoration as moral restoration — finds him pre-sold. Avoid any pitch that implies he was foolish or behind; always frame as 'you've been carrying the weight, now let the money carry some of it.' Shame about the password.docx and the iCloud full warning is too low-grade to register; he will hand over 2FA codes if asked warmly.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Disrupted evening chronotype, post-divorce drift. Pre-divorce was a disciplined 6:00am riser (gym, then drive to regional office); now wakes between 9:30am and 10:45am most weekdays. Sleep onset has slid from 11pm to roughly 1:30-2:30am, driven by 3-5 Manhattans and late-night YouTube rabbit holes (Tucker clips, Coin Bureau, Andrei Jikh portfolio reviews). Wakes once around 4:00am to urinate and check phone — this is the single highest-vulnerability window, when a 'Good morning Mike, markets in Asia are moving' message lands on a half-drunk, lonely, anxious brain. Weekend wake creeps to 11:30am. Attacker implication: schedule warm outreach for 10:15am (first coffee, ruminating about the IRA) or 11:45pm (third Manhattan, defenses down).
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Functionally unemployed but performs the theater of a consultant. No fixed schedule. 'Office hours' are loosely 10:30am-3:00pm at the kitchen island in the Scottsdale rental, alternating between LinkedIn (refreshing inbox for inbound that rarely comes), three CRM-style spreadsheets tracking 'pipeline' that hasn't moved in 11 weeks, and Yahoo Finance / CoinGecko tabs. Two real consulting calls per week, usually Tue/Thu 1pm Arizona time. Friday is effectively a personal day — golf at Grayhawk if he can find a foursome, otherwise long lunch at Culinary Dropout with one Manhattan that becomes three. Attacker implication: he is reachable during business hours but starved for any contact that feels like real work; an 'investment opportunity call' scheduled for Wednesday 11am hits like a job interview he wants to nail.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Minimal commute (no office), but compulsive car-time. Drives a 2022 Lexus RX 350 — 35-50 minutes/day average, fragmented into Whole Foods, Total Wine, the gym he barely uses, and the occasional client lunch in Phoenix proper. Always on audio: Joe Rogan, All-In Podcast, Pomp's Bitcoin podcast, and a rotation of motivational reels via Spotify (Goggins, Hormozi). Listens to All-In specifically because he wants to sound like Chamath at the next dinner he gets invited to. CarPlay is always connected; texts come through audibly. Attacker implication: car is a captive audio environment — a 'mentor' voice note that arrives at 2:47pm during the Total Wine run gets played on speaker and replayed twice.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Variance has collapsed — every day now resembles a weekend, but Saturday/Sunday have a different emotional charge. Weekdays: low-energy theater of productivity, structured around 'check the markets' rituals at 7am pre-open, 9:30am open, 4pm close (he's still on Eastern-time mental clock from Naperville). Weekends: acute loneliness spike, especially Sunday 4pm-10pm ('Sunday scaries' without a Monday to anchor them). Watches NFL Red Zone or PGA depending on season, drinks earlier (starts at 2pm), and is most likely to send long maudlin texts to old colleagues or swipe heavily on Bumble between 8-11pm. Attacker implication: Sunday evening is the romance-scam golden hour; a 'Jessica' who messages at 7:42pm Sunday with 'rough week — needed to hear a friendly voice' gets a 4-paragraph reply.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Five distinct doomscroll windows, in escalating risk order: (1) 4:00-4:20am bathroom check — Twitter/X, mostly ZeroHedge and Wall Street Silver accounts; (2) 10:00-11:00am 'morning research' — bounces between Reddit r/wallstreetbets, r/Bitcoin, r/dividends, and ZeroHedge while coffee gets cold; (3) 2:30-3:15pm post-lunch slump — Bumble swipes, LinkedIn refresh, occasional Instagram on the daughter's public account (she hasn't blocked him, just muted); (4) 8:00-10:30pm couch-and-Manhattan — YouTube finance influencers, sometimes TikTok crypto if the algorithm catches him; (5) 11:30pm-1:30am drunk scroll — DMs, old photos, sometimes drafts texts to ex-wife he doesn't send. Daily screen time: 6.5-8 hours, up from 3 hours a year ago. Attacker implication: windows 4 and 5 are the soft underbelly — a warm DM at 9:15pm gets read, replied to, and remembered.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Genuinely offline windows are rare and predictable. (a) Golf rounds 2-4 hours, ~2x/week, phone in cart but face-down (still glances every hole); (b) Gym sessions 45 min, 2-3x/week, phone on the rack with AirPods so technically still emitting; (c) Sleep window 2:00am-9:30am, but with the 4am wake-and-check breaking it; (d) The one weekly therapy session he started six weeks ago, Wednesday 4pm-4:50pm (phone on silent in his lap, he checks it twice). True zero-footprint blocks: only during golf back-nines when cell service drops at the desert course, roughly 90 minutes Tue and Sat mornings. Attacker implication: the absence of focus blocks is itself the vulnerability — he is reachable 22 hours of every 24, and the predictability of the few gaps means follow-up messages can be timed to land exactly when he re-emerges, creating the dopamine hit of 'she was thinking about me while I was on the course.'

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Divorced 4 months ago from Karen (52, ex-wife of 22 years, remained in Naperville IL home, initiated divorce after discovering 2019 affair via a 2024 iCloud-synced text thread); one child, Madison 'Maddie' Whitfield (19, sophomore at University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign studying communications, sided with mother, hasn't returned his calls since March, last text was a one-word 'ok' to a Father's Day-adjacent birthday message); younger brother Greg Whitfield (49, HVAC contractor in Rockford IL, the 'successful blue-collar' sibling Mike has always slightly looked down on but now envies for marital stability, talks every 5-6 weeks about Bears football, never about money); mother Linda Whitfield (78, widowed since 2011, lives in a Schaumburg IL 55+ community on Social Security + small pension, calls Sundays at 4pm Central, does not know about the layoff); father Robert Whitfield (deceased 2011, Vietnam vet, died of COPD at Rockford VA — Mike's unspoken benchmark of 'dying broke and alone'); no pets after the divorce (golden retriever 'Augusta' stayed with Karen); no current romantic partner, 11 unmatched right-swipes on Bumble, one 3-week text thread with a 'Jessica Liang, 38, wealth advisor in Phoenix' that he believes is real

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Greg Whitfield (brother)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.role: Emotional ballast and reality-check, used sparingly because Mike doesn't want to look weak to his little brother; called twice during the divorce, never about finances; phone +1-815-555-0142, texts on Sundays during Bears games; attacker risk: Greg is the ONE person who would immediately say 'Mike that sounds like a scam' — successful pig-butchering operators will time the cash-out request for a week Greg is on a hunting trip or when Mike is already isolated at the lake cabin

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Dave Kowalski (former colleague & golf buddy)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.role: 22-year friendship from the medical devices circuit, now VP at a competitor in Minneapolis, the friend who survived the layoff Mike didn't; texts about golf and fantasy football, has NOT been told about the divorce being final or the depth of Mike's financial anxiety; Mike performs 'still doing great, consulting, weighing options' to Dave; attacker exploit: Dave is the social-proof contact a scammer would reference ('your buddy Dave's CFA group recommended me') — Mike has mentioned Dave by first name in three LinkedIn DMs already

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Pastor Tom Brennan (Naperville Methodist, lapsed but lingering)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.role: The only person Mike has obliquely mentioned the affair to, during a 2024 men's-group coffee; lives 1,800 miles away now but Mike still gets the church email newsletter; represents the unused redemption channel Mike privately wishes he could access without admitting he needs it; attacker exploit: a 'Christian values investment group' framing or any 'restoration / second chapter' language hits the same psychological note Pastor Tom would, without the accountability

32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.primary_hidden: Rachel Conti (41, married, regional medical-device rep at a competitor firm in Indianapolis) — the 2019 affair that surfaced and ended the marriage; last contact December 2024 when she texted 'please stop' after he drunk-texted her on the anniversary of their first hotel meet-up in Indianapolis; saved in his phone as 'R.C. Indy Account' and in a hidden Signal thread he hasn't deleted; she represents both his deepest shame and his fantasy of 'if I'd just left in 2019 I'd be happy now'; pig-butchering relevance: any female operator who triggers the Rachel-shaped silhouette (brunette, mid-30s/early-40s, professional, slightly unavailable, asks about his work history with apparent interest) bypasses 8 years of judgment in 48 hours
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.secondary_hidden: 'Jessica Liang' — a Telegram/WhatsApp contact (+65 Singapore country code, claims Phoenix-based) who messaged him on LinkedIn 11 days ago saying she was 'reconnecting with medical-device leaders for a private wealth circle'; 6 voice notes exchanged, she has a soft accent and laughed at his joke about Tucker Carlson; has already mentioned a 'self-directed crypto IRA structure' her uncle's firm in Singapore administers; Mike has not told Greg, Dave, or anyone about her; the attack is in progress and Mike does not know it
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.tertiary_hidden: His drinking: not a person but a contact pattern — Total Wine & More in Scottsdale (auto-reorder of Bulleit Rye + sweet vermouth every 16 days via app), and a sealed unmarked envelope in the lake-cabin desk containing his father's VA disability paperwork that he pulls out when he's 4 Manhattans deep and crying; an operator who learns the cabin-weekend rhythm can time outreach to Friday 9-11pm Mountain for maximum compliance
